pageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate - Denica Riadini-Flesch

pageCaption.value: Social entrepreneur Denica Riadini-Flesch has succeeded in creating one of the world’s first regenerative farm-to-closet clothing chains – SukkhaCitta, meaning happiness. Determined to empower rural artisans in Indonesia, she has created hundreds of jobs with fair pay for those who craft high-end clothing using traditional and sustainable techniques.

WaPageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate - Denica Riadini-Flesch

metadescription.value: Entrepreneur Denica Riadini-Flesch is empowering rural Indonesian craftswomen through her farm-to-closet clothing brand, SukkhaCitta. More on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Regenerating land, lives and tradition in Indonesia

text.value:
A part of the Perpetual Planet Initiative, the Rolex Awards for Enterprise support exceptional individuals who have the courage and conviction to take on major challenges, initiating extraordinary projects that make the world a better place.

Regenerating land, lives and tradition in Indonesia

Social entrepreneur Denica Riadini-Flesch has succeeded in creating one of the world’s first regenerative farm-to-closet supply chains – SukkhaCitta, meaning happiness. Determined to empower rural artisans in Indonesia, she has created hundreds of jobs with fair pay for those who craft high-end clothing using traditional and sustainable techniques.

title.value:
Denica Riadini-Flesch: Episode 1
A lineage of craft

pageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate - Denica Riadini-Flesch - Episode 1

pageCaption.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate Denica Riadini-Flesch is reimagining the clothing supply chain through her work empowering rural craftswomen in Indonesia. By way of her farm-to-closet clothing brand SukkhaCitta, the social entrepreneur is reviving indigenous practices and restoring soils, while creating social and economic change in rural communities. Her work embodies the spirit of the Awards, which are part of the Perpetual Planet Initiative. The programme supports individuals with innovative projects to improve life on Earth and preserve cultural and natural heritage for future generations.

WaPageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate - Denica Riadini-Flesch - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Through her clothing brand, Denica Riadini-Flesch is sustaining cultures, providing fair wages and regenerating soils. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate: Denica Riadini-Flesch - Episode 1

pageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate - Denica Riadini-Flesch - Episode 2

pageCaption.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate Denica Riadini-Flesch is demonstrating what can happen when women and nature are placed at the heart of entrepreneurship and innovation. Her clothing brand SukkhaCitta is helping rural women to reclaim and relearn traditional, sustainable farming and textile-manufacturing techniques. Already, the project has changed the lives of more than 1,400 people, from farmers to master weavers and seamstresses. Barren soils are being regenerated while toxic run-off from dyes is being prevented and the connection to the Earth rethreaded.

WaPageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate - Denica Riadini-Flesch - Episode 2

metadescription.value: By applying indigenous farming and dyeing practices, Denica Riadini-Flesch's clothing brand is regenerating the Earth. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate: Denica Riadini-Flesch - Episode 2

pageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate - Denica Riadini-Flesch - Episode 3

pageCaption.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate Denica Riadini-Flesch lifts up rural communities in Indonesia by building craft schools in villages. She created SukkhaCitta, an innovative farm-to-closet social enterprise providing education in indigenous farming and textile-manufacturing techniques, as well as employment and fair pay to women across Indonesia. Artisans are able to work for the company from their villages and have seen an average 60 per cent rise in income, with grants and microcredits awarded to other entrepreneurial women.

WaPageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate - Denica Riadini-Flesch - Episode 3

metadescription.value: Rolex Awards Laureate Denica Riadini-Flesch is scaling up her farm-to-closet social enterprise to reach more women worldwide. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate: Denica Riadini-Flesch - Episode 3

text.value: SukkhaCitta’s work begins with cotton, which Riadini-Flesch ensures is sourced sustainably. Farmers working with the brand are taught indigenous techniques to grow cotton while regenerating the earth. In their fields, complementary crops are planted alongside the cotton, each with its own role in revitalizing the over-worked soils. Once the cotton is harvested, spun and woven by the women, it is coloured using natural, plant-based dyes. “We have prevented over 3 million litres of toxic dyes from being dumped into our rivers by using completely natural dyes,” explains Riadini-Flesch.

SukkhaCitta has also established craft schools in villages where women learn traditional agricultural and textile production techniques alongside business skills that enable them to monetize their work. The brand’s website connects them with customers in 32 countries. Riadini-Flesch says: “This project has shown me what you can achieve when you put women and nature at the heart of your decisions.”

Her key driver is to provide people the opportunities to change their own lives. Women’s financial independence can have a dramatic influence on whole communities, and those working for SukkhaCitta have had on average a 60 per cent increase in income. Through this, Riadini-Flesch has seen education and nutrition improve – and consequently the quality of life – in villages where she works.

Over 1,400 people have been impacted by the project, from farmers to master weavers and seamstresses. Riadini-Flesch plans to reach 10,000 people by 2030 while regenerating 1,000 hectares of degraded soil and is developing an app to extend SukkhaCitta’s training into the most remote regions of Indonesia.

Starting out as a development economist, Riadini-Flesch worked for the World Bank in Indonesia, often travelling to rural villages where she met local women. There she realized work was what was needed, not aid. Together with three women, Riadini-Flesch founded SukkhaCitta. Today, it takes a team of 15 coordinators to manage more than 450 artisans, and the average age of the craftswomen has gone from 60 to 28 years old as younger people begin to see the value of indigenous knowledge.
This project has shown me what you can achieve when you put women and nature at the heart of your decisions.

“These are women who have been invisible for far too long, whose work is being recognized all around the world, whose wisdom is being learned from,” says Riadini-Flesch. Her project is changing the way clothes are made in ways that benefit everyone, from the farmers growing the cotton to the people wearing the finished pieces.

pageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate - Beth Koigi

pageCaption.value: Kenyan entrepreneur Beth Koigi is deploying a piece of technology that is addressing one of the world’s most pressing and complex problems: water scarcity.

WaPageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate - Beth Koigi

metadescription.value: Entrepreneur Beth Koigi is revolutionizing the way clean water is supplied to communities in need through her start-up, Majik Water. More on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate - Inza Koné

pageCaption.value: Inza Koné, Côte d’Ivoire’s first primatologist, has spent almost 20 years working in Tanoé-Ehy, one of West Africa’s last primary rainforests.

WaPageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate - Inza Koné

metadescription.value: Primatologist Inza Koné is leading community-based conservation efforts to protect the biodiversity of Côte d'Ivoire's Tanoé-Ehy forest. More on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate - Liu Shaochuang

pageCaption.value: Remote sensing specialist Liu Shaochuang is trying to save critically endangered wild camels using space technology.

WaPageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate - Liu Shaochuang

metadescription.value: Remote sensing specialist Liu Shaochuang is using space technology to help save the Gobi Desert's critically endangered wild camels. More on rolex.org.
